name: Puerto de Tíscar Loop from Hinojares
description: This difficult 22.9-mile road cycling loop from Hinojares climbs 3087 feet through the Sierra de Cazorla, offering spectacular mountain view
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Hinojares are situated within the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park, a region characterized by an orographical labyrinth of valleys, limestone quarries, gorges, and high plateaus. The landscape combines stunning mountain views with semi-arid areas, offering a distinctive visual experience for cyclists. Significant elevation changes are common, with routes traversing olive groves, forests, and river valleys like those of the Tíscar and Turrillas rivers. This diverse terrain provides a variety of challenging and scenic options for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Hinojares The most popular road cycling route is Granada Geopark: Pozo Alcón and Castril Loop, a 54.9 miles (88.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 16 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain through varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Negratín Reservoir Loop, a difficult 62.8 miles (101.0 km) path. This route offers extensive riding with views of the reservoir and surrounding terrain. Local road cyclists also love the Puerto de Tíscar Loop from Hinojares, a 22.9 miles (36.8 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with significant elevation changes, often completed in about 2 hours 12 minutes. Road cycling around Hinojares is defined by mountainous terrain, semi-arid landscapes, and river valleys, with routes passing through olive groves and forests. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and challenging road bike climbs, catering to different ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 100 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Hinojares's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Hinojares?
A: There are over 15 road cycling routes around Hinojares, offering a diverse range of experiences. These routes are situated within the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park, providing varied terrain from valleys to high plateaus.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Hinojares?
A: Yes, Hinojares offers at least one easy road cycling route. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1344743455/fontanar-y-cuevas-del-campo-circular-desde-pozo-alcon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fontanar y Cuevas del Campo — circular desde Pozo Alcón&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 29.5 km loop with moderate elevation gain, perfect for those new to road cycling in the area.

Q: What are some of the most challenging road cycling routes around Hinojares?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Hinojares offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350371720/granada-geopark-pozo-alcon-and-castril-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Granada Geopark: Pozo Alcón and Castril Loop&lt;/a&gt; covers 88.3 km with over 1200 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1343621484/negratin-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Negratín Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 101 km ride with similar elevation, offering extensive views.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on road cycling routes in Hinojares?
A: Road cycling routes in Hinojares traverse a unique blend of landscapes. You'll experience stunning mountain views, semi-arid areas, olive groves, and pleasant forests. The region is characterized by an 'orographical labyrinth' of valleys, limestone quarries, and gorges, with routes often following river valleys like the Tíscar and Turrillas.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the Hinojares area?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Hinojares are designed as loops. Popular circular routes include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996052243/puerto-de-tiscar-loop-from-hinojares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Tíscar Loop from Hinojares&lt;/a&gt; (36.8 km) and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1343621484/negratin-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Negratín Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; (101 km).

Q: What natural attractions can I see along road cycling routes near Hinojares?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You might encounter features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5303646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of the Virgin of Tíscar and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4838825" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guazalamanco Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3983035" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Guadalquivir River&lt;/a&gt;. The Bolera Reservoir is also a prominent natural feature offering scenic views.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Hinojares?
A: Hinojares is suitable for road cycling for much of the year, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions with milder temperatures. The mountainous terrain can be challenging in summer heat, and some areas may experience colder weather in winter.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the road cycling routes in Hinojares?
A: Yes, the diverse terrain of Hinojares, with its mountains and semi-arid landscapes, offers numerous viewpoints. The Hinojares Viewpoint, for instance, provides views of the unique 'badlands' or Desierto de Hinojares, and many routes offer panoramic vistas of the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park.

Q: Can I find places to rest or stay overnight near the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the area provides options for rest and accommodation. You can find refuges like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3229805" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado Zamora Refuge&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3980301" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Hornico Hostel and Nature Center&lt;/a&gt;, which are part of the network of huts and cabins in the region.

Q: What makes Hinojares a good destination for road cycling?
A: Hinojares is an excellent road cycling destination due to its location within the vast Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park. It offers an 'orographical labyrinth' of diverse terrain, from challenging climbs with gradients up to 13% to scenic roads through olive groves and forests. The region's suitability for cycling is even highlighted by its inclusion in an annual triathlon route.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Hinojares?
A: The komoot community frequently praises the varied and challenging terrain, the stunning natural landscapes, and the quiet, well-maintained paved roads suitable for race bikes. Cyclists often highlight the rewarding descents after significant climbs and the unique blend of mountain and semi-arid scenery.
tips: Viewpoint from where you can see the town of Hinojares, a village sunken into the bottom of a valley and where it is surrounded by the famous "badlands" or Desierto de Hinojares.
The Blue Pilon is between the villages of Don Pedro and Belerda. The waterfall is formed by the Tíscar river, which comes down from the incredible Cueva del Agua and creates this waterfall.

After the steep scene and after having stopped to swim in the Pilon Azul, the last part of the ascent segment is easier. It starts right after leaving Don Pedro. The climb has a length of 1.95 km with a positive slope of 109 m and an average slope of 5.9%.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Puerto de Tíscar Loop from Hinojares?
faqs.answer: This loop is rated as difficult and requires very good fitness. It involves significant climbing, with an elevation gain of approximately 940 meters over its 36.8-kilometer distance. The climb to Puerto de Tíscar itself features sections with gradients up to 13%.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily on paved roads, suitable for racebikes. While the general area can have unpaved sections, the Puerto de Tíscar climb is described as being on generally good, low-traffic roads. You'll encounter varied terrain, including olive groves, pleasant forests, and mountainous sections with fantastic rock formations.

faqs.question: What scenic viewpoints or landmarks can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll be treated to breathtaking landscapes throughout the ride. Key highlights include the Hinojares Viewpoint, the beautiful Pilón Azul Waterfall, and the challenging Trinchera Ascent – Tíscar River. On clear days, you might even catch distant views of the Sierra Nevada mountains.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Puerto de Tíscar Loop from Hinojares?
faqs.answer: Hinojares serves as the starting point for this loop. While specific parking areas for cyclists aren't detailed, you should be able to find suitable street parking within the village of Hinojares to begin your ride. It's a small village, so parking is generally less complicated than in larger towns.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this loop?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Cazorla Natural Park offers beautiful cycling conditions, but the best time depends on your preference. Spring (April-May) and Autumn (September-October) generally provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summers can be very hot, especially in July and August, making early morning rides essential. Winters can be cold, with potential for snow at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle in the Sierra de Cazorla Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, no specific permits or entrance fees are required for road cycling on public roads within the Parque Natural de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected areas, and stay on marked routes. There are no fees for accessing the park itself.

faqs.question: Is the Puerto de Tíscar Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for road cycling, and while dogs are generally allowed in the Sierra de Cazorla Natural Park, bringing a dog on a racebike loop of this difficulty isn't practical. If you plan to visit the park with a dog for other activities, always keep them on a leash and clean up after them, especially in natural areas.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route or in Hinojares?
faqs.answer: Hinojares, as your starting and end point, will have some local establishments where you can find food and drinks. Along the route itself, especially on the more remote sections of the climb, options will be very limited or non-existent. It's highly recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks for the duration of your ride.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several local paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the [CIMA JA05] Puerto de Tíscar * Huesa / Belerda, Sendero de las Salinas del Mesto, Cerro de Hinojares, and Sendero Hinojares - Cuenca, among others. This allows for a varied cycling experience within the region.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this road cycling loop?
faqs.answer: Given the difficulty and length, essential items include a well-maintained racebike, helmet, appropriate cycling attire, sufficient water (at least 2-3 liters), energy snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), sun protection (sunscreen, sunglasses), and a fully charged phone. A GPS device with the route loaded is also highly recommended.

faqs.question: What makes the Puerto de Tíscar climb a classic in the Sierra de Cazorla?
faqs.answer: The Puerto de Tíscar climb is considered a classic due to its significant elevation gain, challenging gradients, and the spectacular, unspoiled natural beauty it traverses. It offers a rewarding experience for experienced road cyclists, combining demanding ascents with breathtaking views of the natural park's unique rock formations and distant mountain ranges.
